svg.aria-label: Synthetic single-token codes vs LAMA factual binding

text.style: fill:#0f172a;font-family:ui-sans-serif, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;font-size:15px;font-weight:600;

svg.text: Figure 2. Same pipeline, two datasets — task-mode wall vs. factual binding success

text.style: fill:#64748b;font-family:ui-sans-serif, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;font-size:11px;font-weight:normal;

svg.text: Held-out top-1 per channel. Left bars: synthetic single-token codes (Stage 6 Phase 1, mean over 8 cells). Right bars: LAMA factual capitals (Stage 6 Phase 2, mean over 3 seeds).

svg.text: Conclusion: the synthetic-data wall (Stage 2C / Stage 7A linear-probe negatives) is task-specific. The same pipeline — answer-token CE + LM-head rank-4 LoRA — reaches the oracle upper bound on real factual data.
